Let f : R →R be a function deﬁned as follows:
f(x) = |x −1| + (x −1)
Which of the following is not true for f?
options: f(x) = f (x′) for all x, x′ < 1
The derivative of f at x = 2 is 2

questions.question_text:
Population of a city is 40% male and 60% female. Suppose also that 50% of males and
30% of females in the city smoke. The probability that a smoker in the city is male is
closest to

A blue and a red die are thrown simultaneously. We deﬁne three events as follows: -
Event E : the sum of the numbers on the two dice is 7 - Event F : the number on the
blue die equals 4 - Event G : the number on the red die equals 3 Which of the following
statements is true?
options: E and F are disjoint events.
E and F are independent events.
E and F are not independent events.
Probability of E is more than the probability of F.

A matrix A ∈Tp is p -special if determinant of A is not divisible by p. How many
matrices in Tp are p -special?

A ”good” word is any seven letter word consisting of letters from {A, B, C} (some letters
may be absent and some letter can be present more than once), with the restriction that
A cannot be followed by B, B cannot be followed by C, and C cannot be followed by A.
How many good words are there?

A random variable X is distributed in [0, 1]. Mr. Fox believes that X follows a distribu-
tion with cumulative density function (cdf) F : [0,1]→[0, 1] and Mr. Goat believes that
X follows a distribution with cdf G : [0, 1] →[0, 1]. Assume F and G are diﬀerentiable,
F ̸= G and F(x) ≤G(x) for all x ∈[0, 1]. Let EF[X] and EG[X] be the expected values
of X for Mr. Fox and Mr. Goat respectively. Which of the following is true?

A fair coin is tossed inﬁnite number of times. The probability that a head turns up for
the ﬁrst time after even number of tosses is

An entrance examination has 10 ”true-false” questions. A student answers all the ques-
tions randomly and his probability of choosing the correct answer is 0.5. Each correct
answer fetches a score of 1 to the student, while each incorrect answer fetches a score of
zero. What is the probability that the student gets the mean score?

The ﬁrst term of an arithmetic progression is a and common diﬀerence is d ∈(0, 1).
Suppose the k -th term of this arithmetic progression equals the sum of the inﬁnite
geometric progression whose ﬁrst term is a and common ratio is d. If a > 2 is a prime
number, then which of the following is a possible value of d?

In period 1, a chicken gives birth to 2 chickens (so, there are three chickens after period
1). In period 2, each chicken born in period 1 either gives birth to 2 chickens or does
not give birth to any chicken. If a chicken does not give birth to any chicken in a period,
it does not give birth in any other subsequent periods. Continuing in this manner, in
period (k + 1), a chicken born in period k either gives birth to 2 chickens or does not
give birth to any chicken. This process is repeated for T periods - assume no chicken
dies. After T periods, there are in total 31 chickens. The maximum and the minimum
possible values of T are respectively

There are 20 persons at a party. Each person shakes hands with some of the persons
at the party. Let K be the number of persons who shook hands with odd number of
persons. What is a possible value of K?

Two independent random variables X and Y are uniformly distributed in the interval
[0, 1]. For a z ∈[0, 1], we are told that probability that max(X, Y ) ≤z is equal to the
probability that min(X, Y ) ≤(1 −z) What is the value of z?

f(x + y)f(x −y) = (f(x) + f(y))2 −4x2f(y)
Which of the following is not possible for f?

is the set of all integers.
f(x) = ⌈x⌉
where ⌈x⌉is the smallest integer that is larger than x. Now, deﬁne a new function g as
follows. For any x ∈R, g(x) = |f(x)| −f(|x|), where |x| gives the absolute value of x.
What is the range of g?
